Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

How to be enthusiastic without being puffed up?

Sadhana · asked by user [] · 2010-11-12 · 2 answers
Hare Krishna.

How can (a beginner) be enthusiastic about the practice, without at the same time being puffed up?

And by "puffed up" I mean both proud, vain as well as as if full of hot air and no substance.

Thank you for your replies.
user [38] · 2010-11-12
Enthusiasm comes either from someone who is enthusiastic, like an infection, or from the inner realization.
user [447] · 2010-11-26
Thank you for your reply, Veda.
I have not forgotten about this thread, I have been thinking about what you said.
